topic_coverage.quoted_user_text: Enable the agent to launch the Categorizer locally so it can run Cursor IDE browser UAT probes by itself; deterministic local dev launch path, Traefik/router or direct-port binding, populated dev-environment.json, end-to-end browser UAT self-test, production deployment untouched.

Docker Compose and container setup, Traefik routing/labels, .cursor/dev-environment.json, docs/engineering/runbook.md UAT/dev launch pointers, docs/engineering/architecture.md runtime connectivity notes, Cursor IDE browser MCP probe wiring.

No application logic changes beyond local launch support; no breaking changes to production Docker Compose services or categorizer.omniflow.cc routing; keep changes local to development/CI workflow and documentation; direct port binding may be simpler than local Traefik; ensure health endpoint GET /health and admin UI / are reachable from Cursor browser on stable local port.

AC-1 .cursor/dev-environment.json declares deterministic local launch command + health URL; AC-2 Docker Compose supports ephemeral local launch without breaking production; AC-3 Traefik/router or direct port binding exposes local service URL reachable from Cursor browser; AC-4 agent triggers launch, waits for health, opens browser probe URL; AC-5 browser probe collects console errors and /api/reviews response evidence; AC-6 user guide documents local agent-driven UAT flow (USER_GUIDE_MODE=1).

Agent can run end-to-end local browser UAT against a launched Categorizer instance; acceptance criteria pass; user guide exists; production deployment config unchanged; no regression in existing production services.

story_summary: Agent-driven local Categorizer launch for Cursor browser UAT probes
